full_suite.failure_notes: No failure exercises the owned trainer or its test module, but none of the 42 is an exact match to the pinned six-failure baseline and they were not all proven to fail at clean HEAD. Classification: 30 concurrent/unowned dirty-file or artifact drift, 10 sandbox/environment failures, and 2 unresolved unowned failures. The repaired parity-premise failure moved to passing.

honest_issues: The full suite is red, so this lane cannot honestly report overall PASS despite meeting all five lane-specific acceptance targets.
The successful parity proof is a 7-step CPU fixture, not the required frozen 2372-step CUDA parity. VERIFIED=0 and DO_NOT_ARM_B2 remain binding.
The shared worktree changed concurrently, including unowned best_stack.json revision 14 to 15. This lane made no best-stack delta or promotion.
The two new parity selector fields serialize as null checkpoint metadata during ordinary training; human compute is invariant, but raw checkpoint bytes differ.
Historical head terminology remains in the parity artifact filename/type for compatibility, and the candidate source is not re-hashed after a long production parity run.
Eight wide failures are sandbox socket-bind denials, two are timing/resource misses, and two unowned failures remain unresolved.
next: Re-pin the candidate SHA, stabilize or separately baseline the shared worktree failures, obtain fresh ultra adversarial review, and run the frozen 2372-step CUDA parity gate before any B2 arming decision.
